questions.question: What latency budget defines "conversational feel" for a voice assistant?
options: Under 5 seconds round trip
Total round-trip under ~500ms — below that users stop noticing lag; GPT-4o claims ~250ms, Moshi ~160ms, Qwen2.5-Omni ~350-500ms, and EVERY component must stream
Exactly one second per response
Latency doesn't matter for voice
questions.explanation: The pipeline — VAD + speech tokenization, optional 2-4 FPS camera input, thinking, then speech synthesis — adds latency at each step. Nothing can be "batch everything then decode."

questions.question: What is the Thinker-Talker decomposition in Qwen2.5-Omni?
options: Two copies of the same model voting

A large (7B-80B) Thinker reasons over interleaved text+image+audio and emits text tokens; a small (200M-1B) Talker converts those into discrete speech tokens; a streaming decoder renders waveform in real time
A planner that writes code for a renderer
Separate models for questions and answers
questions.explanation: The split matters because the jobs differ: reasoning needs scale, text-to-speech-token conversion is local. A bigger Talker isn't more expressive — it's just slower.

questions.question: How do Thinker and Talker run in parallel?
options: Talker waits for the full text response
Talker consumes each text token as Thinker commits it and streams speech tokens immediately — by the time Thinker is at token t+3, audio for t_0..t+2 has already played
They alternate turns every second
Talker predicts the Thinker's output in advance
questions.explanation: Mini-Omni's framing: "talk while thinking in streaming." Thinker and Talker tokens interleave with no batch boundaries — this overlap is what keeps time-to-first-audio-byte inside the budget.

questions.question: What problem does TMRoPE solve inside the Thinker?
options: Reducing the vocabulary size

Vision frames (4 FPS), audio frames (50/s), and text would lose temporal alignment in naive sequence order — TMRoPE assigns absolute timestamps to every token so concurrent events attend as concurrent
Compressing video to fewer tokens
Encrypting the position embeddings
questions.explanation: A vision token at t=2.3s, an audio token at t=2.32s, and the user's "stop" at t=2.35s are seen as the same conceptual moment — the infrastructure for "he waved while saying hello" to work.

questions.question: Which open systems mapped the omni-model space alongside Qwen2.5-Omni?
options: BERT, GPT-2, and T5
Mini-Omni (simplified the interleaved streaming design), Moshi (matched GPT-4o-class latency at ~160ms), and GLM-4-Voice (extended the pattern to Chinese)
Stable Diffusion and Midjourney
AlphaGo and MuZero
questions.explanation: GPT-4o's May 2024 demo set the product target; the open ecosystem spent 2024-2025 racing to that surface, with Qwen2.5-Omni (March 2025) as the reference open design.
